patches.summary: Added seven read-only local SQLite audit commands for triage, net worth, subscriptions, duplicates, stale accounts, recurring misses, and budget burn.
patches.reason: The research manifest lists these novel local-join features as Lunch Money CLI differentiators, but the generator only emitted spec-derived API commands.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli and go build ./... passed after adding the commands.

patches.summary: Added a transactions retag command that previews local matches and bulk-updates category or tag changes.
patches.reason: The generated endpoint commands expose raw transaction updates, but the CLI needs a higher-level local-search workflow for safe bulk retagging.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./... && go vet ./... passed; transactions retag --help shows the requested flags.

patches.summary: Added a changed command that lists locally synced records updated since a cutoff.
patches.reason: Agents need an offline morning-triage affordance that summarizes what changed in the SQLite store after sync.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... && go build ./... && go vet ./... passed; changed help and JSON smoke checks passed.

patches.summary: Adjusted budget burn spend totals to count Lunch Money's canonical transaction tier only.
patches.reason: Expanded local stores can contain both split parents and children or both group parents and children; budget spend should mirror the default API list shape to avoid double-counting.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including split/group budget-burn tier coverage.

patches.summary: Restricted the triage inbox to transactions whose Lunch Money status is unreviewed.
patches.reason: Reviewed or cleared transactions can legitimately remain uncategorized; triage should not show them as pending work solely because category_id is empty.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including reviewed/cleared uncategorized exclusions.

patches.summary: Included crypto-synced accounts when reconstructing historical net worth.
patches.reason: Balance-history entries for crypto_synced sources need matching local accounts; otherwise synced exchange positions are dropped from totals.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including crypto-synced balance-history coverage.

patches.summary: Added and indexed a typed transactions.updated_at column for changed-since queries.
patches.reason: Filtering transactions with json_extract(data, '$.updated_at') forces a full table scan; an indexed column keeps changed --since bounded by the updated_at index.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/store passes, including query-plan coverage for idx_transactions_updated_at.

patches.summary: Filtered split and group child rows out of subscription cadence grouping.
patches.reason: Split/group children create same-day duplicate merchant rows that distort inter-charge gaps and can hide real subscriptions.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including split/group child subscription cadence coverage.

patches.summary: Prevented recurring missing fallback matching from reusing transactions linked to a different recurring item.
patches.reason: When two recurring items share identical payee and amount criteria, a transaction already linked to one item should not satisfy the other through fallback matching.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including linked and unlinked recurring transaction matching coverage.

patches.summary: Normalized indexed transaction updated_at values to a fixed-width UTC timestamp.
patches.reason: SQLite compares TEXT index values lexicographically; mixing RFC3339 and space-separated timestamp formats can silently exclude recent changed-since results.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli and go test ./internal/store pass, including mixed-format changed-since and v3 migration coverage.

patches.summary: Filtered split/group transactions out of duplicate clusters at the member level.
patches.reason: A handled split or group transaction can share a merchant/date/amount cluster with genuine duplicates; excluding the whole cluster hides the duplicate charges.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including duplicate clustering with a handled split member.

patches.summary: Routed novel command JSON and table output through Cobra's command writer.
patches.reason: The --deliver buffer is attached to cmd.OutOrStdout(); direct os.Stdout and fmt.Print calls bypass file and webhook delivery sinks.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including command-output routing coverage for changed --json.

patches.summary: Filtered retag updates to canonical editable transaction rows and excluded unconverted foreign balances from net-worth totals.
patches.reason: Bulk transaction updates should not target split parents or group children, and aggregate net-worth totals must not add raw non-base-currency balances when no to_base conversion is available.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including retag split/group filtering and unconverted foreign-balance net-worth coverage.

patches.summary: Derived retag updated counts and row errors from the bulk update API response.
patches.reason: The retag command should not report every matched transaction as updated when the API returns fewer updated rows or per-row errors.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including retag response count and error parsing coverage.

patches.summary: Restricted HTTP cache permissions and excluded split children from bulk retag updates.
patches.reason: Cached Lunch Money API responses contain financial data and should be owner-only, and split children carry deliberate per-split categories that bulk retag should not overwrite.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/client and go test ./internal/cli pass for cache permission and retag split-child coverage.

patches.summary: Created local SQLite store directories and files with owner-only permissions.
patches.reason: The local SQLite store contains synced financial data and should not be readable or traversable by other local users.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/store passes, including store directory and database artifact permission coverage.

patches.summary: Created export, deliver, and generated cache files with owner-only permissions.
patches.reason: Local command output and cached API responses can include Lunch Money transactions, balances, and account metadata.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cache and go test ./internal/cli pass for private cache, export, and deliver file permissions.

patches.summary: Wired captured internal Plaid fetch, budget write, CSV import, import-config, and multipart transaction helper endpoints.
patches.reason: The generated public OpenAPI surface misses or cannot authenticate several web-UI internal endpoints needed for CLI parity.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/internalapi and go test ./internal/cli pass for captured paths and command wiring.

patches.summary: Serialized multi-rule apply calls so each criteria_id is sent to /rules/apply in its own request.
patches.reason: The internal backend can misapply batched commit requests by routing all matched transactions through the final rule's action.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes, including a regression test for serialized ApplyRules calls.

patches.summary: Kept --agent and machine-format command streams parseable by rendering process errors as JSON and suppressing dry-run previews.
patches.reason: Generated endpoint commands returned human stderr on API failures and dry-runs, breaking common `2>&1 | jq` agent pipelines.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes, and bad category create plus dry-run pipelines both parse through jq.

patches.summary: Made rules update preserve existing rule fields when only action flags are supplied.
patches.reason: The internal PUT endpoint requires a full rule body, but CLI callers naturally expect action-only updates such as category and notes edits to keep the existing payee/match condition.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes, including command-level coverage that rules update preserves payee/match while changing category and notes.

patches.summary: Rendered errors as JSON whenever stdout is piped, even without --agent.
patches.reason: Generated commands auto-select JSON for piped success output, but API failures still printed human text and broke `2>&1 | jq` transaction-update pipelines.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes, including a transactions update --stdin regression for piped API errors.

patches.summary: Allowed transactions retag to set notes while applying local-match bulk updates.
patches.reason: Agents were falling back to brittle raw sqlite and hand-built JSON payloads when a category reframing also needed notes.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including retag payload and dry-run JSON coverage for notes.

patches.summary: Fixed mutation safety, generated request fidelity, local cache correctness, pagination, structured dry-runs, and MCP/output hygiene.
patches.reason: Review found several CLI paths that could mutate unexpectedly, drop declared parameters, lose explicit clear values, truncate cached data, or emit output that agents could not audit safely.
patches.validated_outcome: Targeted regression tests pass for each issue cluster; full-suite verification recorded in the working branch.

patches.summary: Made the repository's golangci-lint v2 configuration and current generated tree pass cleanly.
patches.reason: The previous config unintentionally inherited default linters and exposed generated-model noise, stale dead helpers, import-order drift, and small staticcheck issues.
patches.validated_outcome: golangci-lint config verify and golangci-lint run pass with 0 issues.

patches.summary: Routed analytics, tail, sync, and idempotent no-op machine output through the CLI delivery path.
patches.reason: PR #551 exposed direct stdout writes as a review failure class; local review found the same class outside the novel command set.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including analytics --deliver, sync writer, and no-op delivery buffer coverage.

patches.summary: Made internal request return typed API errors instead of exiting the process from command code.
patches.reason: Commands must let Execute, tests, MCP wrappers, and delivery cleanup observe failures instead of terminating the host process directly.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes; static scan finds no os.Exit calls inside internal/cli.

patches.summary: Added LUNCHMONEY_INTERNAL_COOKIE as a non-persistent internal API cookie auth override.
patches.reason: CI and short-lived shell sessions need internal API auth without writing browser session cookies into the persisted jar.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/internalapi passes, including env-cookie parsing and non-persistence coverage.

patches.summary: Collapsed the public-API auth env-var surface to a single canonical LUNCHMONEY_API_KEY and dropped the two operational env vars (LUNCH_MONEY_CONFIG, LUNCH_MONEY_BASE_URL). Trimmed manifest.json user_config to one required field so MCP clients do not demand the token five times.
patches.reason: Generator's OR-case auth pattern emitted five aliases (LUNCHMONEY_ACCESS_TOKEN + four ecosystem aliases for lunchable/lunchtui/robshox MCP) for one bearer token, plus two operational env vars. User wanted minimal surface and did not need ecosystem-compat aliases. The four alias env vars and matching Config fields collapse to a single AccessToken field. LUNCH_MONEY_CONFIG was redundant with the existing --config flag. LUNCH_MONEY_BASE_URL was only used by tests for httptest server URLs and by the mock.lunchmoney.dev development path; tests now write base_url to a temp config TOML and pass --config <path> instead. Internal-API LUNCHMONEY_INTERNAL_COOKIE is unrelated and untouched. Closes Greptile finding #1 on PR #624 (5x required: true).
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./... pass.

patches.summary: Filtered status=exclude transactions out of the budget burn-down spent total.
patches.reason: Lunch Money excludes status=exclude rows from budget calculations; the local burn-down was inflating spent and the over/under projection by counting them. Closes Greptile finding #2 on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes.

patches.summary: Taught splitShellArgs to honor POSIX single-quoted grouping, not just double-quoted.
patches.reason: MCP callers passing a value containing a space via single quotes (e.g. 'Bob's diner') saw their token silently split into multiple positional arguments. Closes Greptile finding #3 on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/mcp/cobratree passes, including single-quote and mixed-quote regression coverage.

patches.summary: Replaced ftsRowID polynomial hash with FNV-1a 64-bit; injected a NUL boundary between scope and id.
patches.reason: The h*31 polynomial mixed poorly, leaving a measurable collision rate on FTS5 rowids; a collision would cause DELETE WHERE rowid=? before re-insert to delete the wrong resource's FTS entry and silently miss search hits. Closes Greptile finding #4 on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/store passes (existing search/index tests cover the path).

patches.summary: Extended the status=exclude filter to subscriptionCountsTransaction and duplicateCountsTransaction.
patches.reason: The earlier budget-burn fix only covered budgets_burn; subscription cadence inference and duplicate clustering still let excluded rows in, so excluded charges polluted those reports. Mirroring the same guard keeps the three novel commands consistent. Closes Greptile re-review on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes.

patches.summary: Guarded recurringExpectedDate against unknown granularity values from the API.
patches.reason: addRecurringCadence silently returned the input on an unrecognized granularity string, sending the surrounding loop into 10000 no-op iterations before dropping the recurring item from the report with no signal. Pre-check via isKnownRecurringGranularity so an unknown value short-circuits cleanly. Closes Greptile re-review on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes; addRecurringCadence default branch becomes unreachable via the guard.

patches.summary: Extended the status=exclude filter to retagCountsTransaction and recurringHasTransaction; added Status to retagTransaction.
patches.reason: retag mutates rows via the API, so an excluded row must not be matched by a broad --match regex; recurringHasTransaction must not let an excluded historical match suppress a genuine missed-payment alert. Closes Greptile second re-review on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes.

patches.summary: Reordered resolveRetagTagIDs to validate --remove-tag names before POSTing any --add-tag tags.
patches.reason: Original order ran addSpecs (createMissing=true, POSTs /tags) before removeSpecs (validate-only). A --remove-tag typo after a successful --add-tag created orphan tags in Lunch Money — server-persisted but never associated with any transaction. Closes the remaining Greptile P1 thread on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes.

patches.summary: Excluded split parents and group children from isUnreviewedForTriage.
patches.reason: Triage was the only counts predicate that did not align with budget burn / subscription / duplicate / retag on the canonical-transaction-tier rule. A single split receipt produced one inbox entry per child plus a duplicate for the parent total, polluting suggested-category confidence. Closes Greptile fourth re-review P1 on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes.

patches.summary: Restricted 5xx retry to idempotent HTTP methods (GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS).
patches.reason: Original code retried every 5xx regardless of method. createRetagTag POSTs /tags and a 5xx after server-side commit would, on retry, create a duplicate tag. Now POST and PATCH return the 5xx without retry; caller can surface a clean error and the user re-decides. Closes Greptile fourth re-review P1 on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/client passes.

patches.summary: buildNetWorth errors out when primaryCurrency is empty instead of silently returning a near-zero total.
patches.reason: loadPrimaryCurrency returns ("", nil) when the profile resource has not been synced. The downstream netWorthBalance inBase check then never fires for same-currency manual accounts, so result.Total only picks up foreign-currency Plaid accounts with to_base set. The total looked wrong with no signal to the user. Surface the missing sync as a clear error directing the user to run sync. Closes Greptile fifth re-review P1 on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes.

patches.summary: Internal-API 401 token-refresh retry gated on idempotent methods, mirroring the public-client 5xx gate.
patches.reason: doWithEncodedBody used to retry the original request after a 401-triggered token refresh regardless of method. 401s typically fire before body processing, so a POST retry is normally safe, but a token expiring mid-handler could leave a successful POST followed by a 401, and the retry would double-commit. Limit auto-retry to GET/PUT/DELETE/HEAD/OPTIONS; non-idempotent callers surface the 401 and re-run if appropriate. Pre-emptive fix for the pattern Greptile would likely flag on its next pass.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/internalapi passes.

patches.summary: Excluded synthetic group-parent transactions from triage and bulk retag selection.
patches.reason: Group parents have no group_parent_id, so child-only filters let aggregate rows into triage voting and bulk-retag API updates. Mirroring the canonical transaction-tier rule keeps synthetic totals out of user-facing mutation and review workflows. Closes Greptile P1 threads on PR #624.
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cli passes, including group-parent exclusions for triage and retag.

deferred_to_upstream.topic: Generator OR-case alias absorption should be opt-in, not default
deferred_to_upstream.note: The generator's research/absorb phase added env-var aliases for neighbor tools (lunchable, lunchtui, robshox MCP) without user opt-in. Consider a manifest knob (e.g. auth_compat: minimal | ecosystem) so future prints pick the surface explicitly instead of forcing a post-generation trim.
